title: eschscholzia californica subsp. mexicana
intro: Eschscholzia californica subsp. mexicana, also known as Mexican California poppy or golden poppy, is a subspecies of flowering plant native to the southwestern United States and northern Mexico. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antispasmodic effects, which can help alleviate symptoms of anxiety, insomnia, and muscle pain. The plant's roots and flowers can be used to prepare various herbal preparations, such as teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used to promote relaxation, improve sleep quality, and reduce stress levels. Additionally, the plant's extracts have been shown to exhibit antioxidant and antimicrobial properties, further solidifying its potential health benefits and medicinal uses.
